sanskrit: दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च । अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम् ॥
transliteration: dambho darpo ’bhimānaś ca krodhaḥ pāruṣyam eva ca ajñānaṁ cābhijātasya pārtha sampadam āsurīm
literal_meaning: Hypocrisy, arrogance, conceit, anger, harshness and ignorance— these qualities belong to one born with demoniac endowment, O Pārtha.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists six core traits that form a ‘demoniac’ personality structure: a false front (dambha), inflated self-worth (darpa/abhimāna), reactive hostility (krodha), interpersonal cruelty (pāruṣya), and fundamental unawareness (ajñāna). Collectively they constitute an entrenched characterological defense against vulnerability and an over-compensatory grab for power.
psychological_state: Narcissistic armor—an early-adopting, self-righteous schema that masks core shame with grandiosity, moral disengagement and reactive anger.
trigger_scenario: A manager publicly humiliates a junior for a minor error, then claims credit for the team’s success in the same meeting.
anchor_text: Notice hypocrisy, arrogance, anger, cruelty, and ignorance—then choose humility, clarity, and compassion instead.
